editionData.title: Daily Brief for May 25, 2026: Russia’s overnight barrage on Ukraine includes use of powerful Oreshnik missile; Africa is Catholicism’s future, but...
editionData.description: Global headlines: Russia’s overnight barrage on Ukraine includes use of powerful Oreshnik missile (At least two people died in Kyiv, which faced the brunt of the attack, Ukrainian officials said. It was one of the biggest overnight aerial assaults since the beginning of Russia's invasion); Africa is Catholicism’s future, but Pentecostal churches are growing faster (The growth of Christianity in Africa has turned the continent into a denominational battleground. Pope Leo’s visit to Angola came as his church confronts a rising challenge); and Studying sports and disability, he laid the groundwork for the Special Olympics (Frank Hayden, who has died at 96, saw sports as a gateway to opportunity for people with special needs). Markets leaned higher, with 6 of 7 tracked indexes up; Nikkei 225 up 2.85% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: Show HN: Audiomass – a free, open-source multitrack audio editor for the web (AudioMass is a free full-featured web-based audio & waveform editing tool); DeepSeek reasonix, DeepSeek native coding agent with high caching and low cost (Open-source AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around DeepSeek); and A fundamental principle of aeronautical engineering has been overturned (It’s long been accepted that the smoother the surface, the lower the aerodynamic drag. That turns out not always to be the case). Product Hunt features Stitch 3.0 by Google (Generate and iterate UI screens with AI on a live canvas), DockFlow (Save, switch, and automate Dock layouts for every workflow), and ModelHub (The missing menu bar app for local LLMs on Mac).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Russia’s overnight barrage on Ukraine includes use of powerful Oreshnik missile
headlines.summary: At least two people died in Kyiv, which faced the brunt of the attack, Ukrainian officials said. It was one of the biggest overnight aerial assaults since the beginning of Russia's invasion.

headlines.title: Africa is Catholicism’s future, but Pentecostal churches are growing faster
headlines.summary: The growth of Christianity in Africa has turned the continent into a denominational battleground. Pope Leo’s visit to Angola came as his church confronts a rising challenge.

headlines.title: Studying sports and disability, he laid the groundwork for the Special Olympics
headlines.summary: Frank Hayden, who has died at 96, saw sports as a gateway to opportunity for people with special needs.

headlines.title: The U.S. and Iran work toward deal to extend ceasefire and reopen Hormuz
headlines.summary: The countries have developed a memorandum of understanding “framework” as the two sides attempt to reach a final deal."

headlines.title: NATO allies grapple with U.S. pullback, confusion over its military plans
headlines.summary: As Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with NATO foreign ministers in Sweden, the focus is on the U.S. moving to reduce its military footprint in Europe.

headlines.title: Building collapse in Philippines leaves 3 dead, 17 missing
headlines.summary: Rescuers search for survivors after thermal scans show signs of breathing and heartbeats beneath debris.

headlines.title: Uncertainty persists as Trump says Iran deal not ‘fully negotiated’
headlines.summary: US president says any potential agreement with Tehran will be 'good and proper' as mediation efforts continue.

headlines.title: Ecuador’s Noboa pledges to extradite criminals in State of the Union speech
headlines.summary: The right-wing president highlighted anti-crime operations and economic progress, while critics warned of abuses.

headlines.title: Cuba thanks China for rice shipment amid worsening humanitarian conditions
headlines.summary: The Caribbean island has faced a fuel blockade that has cut off essential supplies, as Trump seeks regime change.

headlines.title: After San Diego shooting, Muslim Americans aim to turn grief into action
headlines.summary: More than 25,000 US Muslims have come together for the annual ICNA conference, where speakers encouraged activism.

headlines.title: California chemical tank has cracked causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
headlines.summary: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

headlines.title: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
headlines.summary: Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.

headlines.title: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
headlines.summary: The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?

headlines.title: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
headlines.summary: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

headlines.title: Religious leaders, lawmakers push for $1 billion to secure houses of worship
headlines.summary: There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.

headlines.title: Experts endorse unified plan to tackle Ebola outbreak
headlines.summary: The Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has launched a coordinated response with African countries to contain the worsening Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo amid growing fears that misinformation, insecurity and porous borders could accelerate the spread of the deadly virus.

headlines.title: Gunman shot dead near White House
headlines.summary: WASHINGTON — A gunman opened fire near the White House on Saturday evening, with US Secret Service agents killing the assailant in a shoot-out during which a bystander was also struck, authorities said.

headlines.title: US, Iran inch closer to deal on ending war
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran have both indicated they are close to reaching a final deal in their memorandum of understanding, but trod cautiously amid looming threats of ongoing conflict from both sides.

headlines.title: Long-standing Sino-Pakistani relationship valued
headlines.summary: As China and Pakistan marked the 75th anniversary of diplomatic ties on Thursday, political leaders, diplomats and analysts in Pakistan have highlighted the longstanding, multifaceted Sino-Pakistani relationship, emphasizing its economic, social, cultural and diplomatic dimensions.

headlines.title: Global view of China grows more positive
headlines.summary: China is viewed positively across much of the world, a recent study has said, with experts attributing it to the country's governance achievements.

headlines.title: Huawei proposes new path for chip development amid US sanctions
headlines.summary: Reuters: Huawei proposes new path for chip development amid US sanctions.

headlines.title: Forensic experts sift through ruined dormitory in Russian-held Luhansk region
headlines.summary: Reuters: Forensic experts sift through ruined dormitory in Russian-held Luhansk region.

headlines.title: Possible deal on Iran divides US lawmakers largely along party lines
headlines.summary: Reuters: Possible deal on Iran divides US lawmakers largely along party lines.

headlines.title: Protesters in Spain condemn police handling of Gaza flotilla activists
headlines.summary: Reuters: Protesters in Spain condemn police handling of Gaza flotilla activists.

headlines.title: Netanyahu told Trump Israel will remain free to act against threats, Israeli source says
headlines.summary: Reuters: Netanyahu told Trump Israel will remain free to act against threats, Israeli source says.

hackerNews.title: A fundamental principle of aeronautical engineering has been overturned
hackerNews.summary: It’s long been accepted that the smoother the surface, the lower the aerodynamic drag. That turns out not always to be the case.

hackerNews.title: Memory has grown to nearly two-thirds of AI chip component costs
hackerNews.summary: High-bandwidth memory (HBM) accounts for 63% of AI chip component costs, up from 52% in Q1 2024. Epoch AI

hackerNews.title: Constraint Decay: The Fragility of LLM Agents in Back End Code Generation
hackerNews.summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents demonstrate strong performance in autonomous code generation under loose specifications. However, production-grade software requires strict adherence to structural constraints, such as architectural patterns, databases, and object-relational mappings. Existing benchmarks often overlook these non-functional requirements, rewarding functionally correct but structurally arbitrary solutions. We present a systematic study evaluating how well agents handle structural constraints in multi-file backend generation. By fixing a unified API contract across 80 greenfield generation tasks and 20 feature-implementation tasks spanning eight web frameworks, we isolate the effect of structural complexity using a dual evaluation with end-to-end behavioral tests and static verifiers. Our findings reveal a phenomenon of constraint decay: as structural requirements accumulate, agent performance exhibits a substantial decline. Capable configurations lose 30 points on average in assertion pass rates from baseline to fully specified tasks, while some weaker configurations approach zero. Framework sensitivity analysis exposes significant performance disparities: agents succeed in minimal, explicit frameworks (e.g., Flask) but perform substantially worse on average in convention-heavy environments (e.g., FastAPI, Django). Finally, error analysis identifies data-layer defects (e.g., incorrect query composition and ORM runtime violations) as the leading root causes. This work highlights that jointly satisfying functional and structural requirements remains a key open challenge for coding agents.

hackerNews.title: Microsoft open-sources “the earliest DOS source code discovered to date”
hackerNews.summary: Old 86-DOS source code dates back to the time before Microsoft bought it.

hackerNews.title: Mastering Dyalog APL
hackerNews.summary: The “ Mastering Dyalog APL ” book is the de facto standard for people who are looking to learn Dyalog APL from a book. In today’s world technology changes rapidly, so a printed book about a programming language is at risk of becoming outdated.

hackerNews.title: Defeating Git Rigour Fatigue with Jujutsu
hackerNews.summary: When developing a large feature, writing Good Commits is hard.

hackerNews.title: Scammers are abusing an internal Microsoft account to send spam links
hackerNews.summary: The loophole allows spammers and scammers to send emails from a legitimate Microsoft email address typically used for sending genuine account alerts.
